metadata.title: Bizarre Lineage Codes - Verified Reward Tracker
metadata.description: Track Bizarre Lineage codes with a verification-first format covering active status, expired rewards, redemption steps, and troubleshooting notes.

hero.title: Bizarre Lineage Codes with verification-first notes
hero.description: This MVP page is designed to surface usable rewards quickly. When a code is not freshly verified, the page says so instead of pretending it is current.

introduce.title: Current page structure
introduce.description: The live content model is built for fast scans first. Replace the placeholder entries below with fresh checks as soon as you validate them in game.

items.title: Active code slot
items.description: No newly verified code is published in this MVP seed yet. Add a real code only after confirming reward and expiry behavior.

items.title: Reward field
items.description: Record the exact reward text players receive instead of broad guesses like free items or boosts.

items.title: Status field
items.description: Use clear labels such as Verified Active, Unconfirmed, or Expired so players know how much to trust the entry.

items.title: Last checked field
items.description: Show the review date whenever you update the page so the reader understands page freshness immediately.

benefits.title: What this page should answer fast
benefits.description: The codes page exists for urgency. A player should know what works, what failed, and what to try next without scrolling through generic filler.

items.title: Which codes are worth trying right now
items.description: Keep valid rewards at the top and move expired entries out of the way instead of mixing everything together.

items.title: How to redeem without guessing
items.description: Explain the in-game entry point in short steps and note that capitalization or spacing can matter.

items.title: Why a code may fail
items.description: Common failure causes include expiry, syntax mismatch, one-time use limits, or patch-related disablement.

features.title: Redemption and troubleshooting checklist
features.description: Use this section as the durable part of the page even when no new code is verified that day.

items.title: Launch the game and open the code entry interface
items.description: If the interface moves after an update, revise this step instead of leaving old directions live.

items.title: Paste the code exactly as shown
items.description: Do not normalize capitalization or spacing unless you have confirmed the game ignores formatting differences.

items.title: Redeem one code at a time
items.description: This makes it easier to confirm which reward succeeded and which code has already expired.

items.title: Move failed entries into the expired section only after a recheck
items.description: Treat a single failure report as a signal, not final proof, until you test it again.

items.title: Link out to Tier List and Beginner Guide
items.description: Codes are usually a starting point. Users often need progression and stand advice immediately after redeeming rewards.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.description: Short answers to typical code-page questions.

items.question: Why does this page not list many codes yet?
items.answer: The MVP prefers verified entries over filler. Real codes should be added only after they are checked in game.

items.question: How often should the codes page be updated?
items.answer: Update it whenever the game receives an event, patch, or community-reported code drop, and stamp the page with the last review date.

items.question: What should happen to expired codes?
items.answer: Keep them in a clearly separated expired section so players can confirm they are old without polluting the active list.

cta.title: Need the next decision after codes?
cta.description: Most players redeem rewards and then ask what to build or farm next. Continue into the Tier List or Beginner Guide.
